fault_hypothesis: The overlap condition on line 73 uses strict inequalities (`end <= s` and `start >= e`), which allows adjacent slots (where end==s or start==e) to pass through without conflict. The condition should use strict less-than (`end < s` and `start > e`) to also reject adjacent slots.

constraints.intent: After the overlap check, adjacent slots (end==s or start==e) should have been caught as conflicts, so neither equality should hold when we continue past the check.
At the end of each loop iteration that did not return False, the new slot must be strictly non-adjacent to the current existing slot (s, e).

spec.expr: all(b[0] > a[1] for a, b in zip(result__1, result__1[1:]))

constraints.intent: After building the result, every consecutive pair of slots must have a strict gap (no adjacency), confirming adjacent slots were rejected.

spec.expr: not (__cbfl_result[0] and any(b[0] <= a[1] for a, b in zip(__cbfl_result[1], __cbfl_result[1][1:])))

constraints.intent: If insertion succeeded, no two consecutive slots in the returned schedule should be adjacent or overlapping.
